Name: Sita
Effect: On enter: Move this card to the opponent's side of this location. If a friendly hero is at this location at the end of your turn, move Sita back to your side and draw two cards.
Anecdote: 'The ten-headed king carried her through the sky in his chariot. She dropped her jewels to the earth below, one by one, as a trail.'

Name: Jatayu, the Vulture King
Anecdote: 'The old eagle rose against the flying chariot and broke its wings — and fell. With his last breath he told Rama where she had been taken.'

Name: Vanara Scouts
Effect: On enter: Draw 'Hanuman, Son of the Wind' from your deck.
Anecdote: 'The monkey-host searched the four quarters of the earth. Only the south remained — and the sea.'

Name: The Golden Deer
Effect: On enter: Move an enemy hero at this location to another location.
Anecdote: 'No deer like it had ever walked the forest, golden-hided and silver-spotted. Catch it for me, said Sita. It was Maricha, and it was a trap.'

Name: Vibhishana, the Righteous Brother
Effect: On enter: If an opponent has a demon in play, draw two cards.
Anecdote: 'He counselled Ravana to return the stolen queen, and was kicked from the council. So he crossed the sea and knelt before Rama.'

Name: Vanara Host
Anecdote: 'Bears and monkeys beyond counting tore up trees and mountains for their weapons and roared at the sea.'

Name: Rama Setu, the Floating Bridge
Effect: While on top: Once per turn, you may move a friendly being from another location to this location.
Anecdote: 'They wrote his name on the stones, and the stones floated. In five days the sea was crossed.'

Name: Lakshmana, the Devoted
Effect: While on top: Enemy card effects cannot move or return your beings at this location.
Anecdote: 'He drew a line around the hut: Stay within it, and no power of the three worlds can touch you. She crossed it.'

Name: Sugriva, the Monkey King
Effect: On enter: You may play another Vanara from your hand for free.
Anecdote: 'When Sugriva roared from the mountain, the whole monkey nation came — from the caves, from the forests, from the ends of the earth.'

Name: Hanuman, Son of the Wind
Effect: While on top: Once per turn, you may move Hanuman to another location. If 'Sita' is at that location, draw two cards.
Anecdote: 'He grew vast as a mountain, crouched, and leapt the hundred leagues of sea in a single bound, his shadow racing over the waves.'

Name: Garuda, the Divine Eagle
Anecdote: 'The serpent-arrows bound the brothers fast. Then the sky darkened with wings, and every snake fled the field.'

Name: Indrajit, the Unseen
Effect: Enemy card effects cannot target Indrajit.
Anecdote: 'Thrice he rose invisible into the clouds and rained arrows on the host. No eye could find him; no weapon could follow.'

Name: Rama, the Exiled Prince
Effect: On enter: For each opponent, destroy an enemy being at this location with Power [4] or less.
Anecdote: 'He strung the bow of Shiva that no other man could lift, and it broke with a sound like thunder.'

Name: Kumbhakarna, the Sleeping Giant
Effect: On enter: Flip this card face-down; it has -6 Power. At the start of your turn, you may flip it face-up.
Anecdote: 'A thousand elephants walked over him and he did not stir. When at last he woke, he was hungry.'

Name: Ravana, the Ten-Headed
Effect: On enter: Gain control of an enemy being with a cost of [2] or less at this location.
Anecdote: 'Ten heads, twenty arms, and a boon against gods and demons — but he did not think to ask for protection from men and monkeys.'
